# How to plan delivery

Planning a delivery is straightforward when you have a checklist.
This guide walks through the steps for **how to plan delivery** for
small businesses.

![Delivery checklist diagram](https://example.com/img/checklist.png)

## Why plan a delivery in advance?

Last-minute delivery scheduling causes most of the cost overruns we
see. Spending ten minutes on a plan saves hours of rework later.

## Step-by-step planning

1. Pick the delivery window.
2. Confirm the address and contact.
3. Choose a [local delivery service](https://example.com/services/local-delivery/).
4. Confirm pricing on the [pricing page](https://example.com/pricing/).

## Common pitfalls when you plan delivery

Most teams forget to confirm the contact phone number, then lose half
an hour to a stalled delivery on arrival.

## Next steps

Read the [delivery cost guide](https://example.com/blog/delivery-cost-guide/)
for an estimation walkthrough, then review the
[long-haul delivery service](https://example.com/services/long-haul-delivery/)
options if your distance is over 200 km.
